question: Which of the following best describes a 'static' method in Java?
options: A method that belongs to the class itself, not to any specific object instance
A method that cannot be changed or overridden
A method that is always available for use
A method that is used to initialize static variables
explanation: A static method is associated with the class itself, rather than with any specific instance (object) of that class. This means you can call it using the class name without creating an object.

The static variable 'count' is shared by all instances of the class. Each time the constructor is called (for each new object), 'count' is incremented. Two objects are created, so the final value is 2.

question: Which statement about a static block in Java is correct?
options: It is executed when an object of the class is created.
It is executed before the main method when the class is loaded into memory.
It can access non-static (instance) variables of the class.
It is used to define constants.
explanation: A static block is used to initialize static data members and is executed exactly once when the class is first loaded into the JVM, which happens before the main method is called or any instance of the class is created.

question: What is a key restriction on a static method?
options: It cannot be declared as public.
It cannot call other static methods.
It cannot directly access instance variables or call non-static methods.
explanation: A static method exists independently of any object instances. Therefore, it cannot directly access instance variables or call non-static methods because those require an object instance to operate on. It can only directly access other static members.

Line C will cause an error. A static method cannot access instance variables (like 'instanceVar') because it is called without an object instance. The static method can only directly access other static members (like 'staticVar' on Line D).
